items.title: Pick by intent, not type
items.avoid: Using a tuple just because 'it's faster'.
items.prefer: Tuple for fixed-size records and hashable keys; list for collections that grow.
items.why: Picking by intent communicates the contract to readers and tools (and to type checkers).
items.example:
```python
shape: tuple[int, int, int] = (3, 224, 224)
batch: list[Tensor] = []
```

items.title: Name your fields once positions matter
items.avoid: Long positional tuples like `(model, ts, n_prompt, n_comp, cost)`.
items.example:
```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CallRecord:
    model: str
    ts: float
    n_prompt: int
    n_completion: int
    cost: float
```

items.title: Build hashable keys carefully
items.avoid: Putting mutable objects inside a tuple meant to be a dict key.
items.prefer: Convert each inner mutable to a tuple / frozenset / str.
items.why: One mutable element makes the whole tuple unhashable.

items.title: Use unpacking for clarity
items.prefer: Unpack into named variables: `x, y = pt`.
items.why: Reads like English; less chance of off-by-one mistakes.
